proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, feature_014 status is associated with pfs_months: patients with feature_014=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those with feature_014=0.
Among the binary features, feature_027=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_027=0 (suggesting feature_027 is a candidate beneficial intervention or favorable biomarker).
Among the binary features, feature_023=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023=0.
Among the binary features, feature_006=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006=0.

The remaining binary features (feature_002, feature_010, feature_011, feature_012, feature_016, feature_018, feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_028, feature_031) each have no detectable mean-difference in pfs_months between their two levels at p<0.05.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_014=0 -> 5.055, feature_014=1 -> 3.703. Welch t-test p effectively 0; the binary variable explains a very large prognostic gap.
Mean pfs_months: feature_027=0 -> 4.117, feature_027=1 -> 5.089. Welch t-test p=1.7e-216; strong positive marginal association.
Mean pfs_months: feature_023=0 -> 4.448, feature_023=1 -> 4.121. p=1.9e-58.
Mean pfs_months: feature_006=0 -> 4.419, feature_006=1 -> 4.114. p=1.0e-49.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for each remaining binary feature.
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 (-0.228, p=3.5e-7) and feature_011 (+0.221, p=7.3e-4) reach significance. feature_002, feature_010, feature_012, feature_016, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_028, feature_031 all have p>0.05 (smallest p=0.088 for feature_024). The 'no effect' hypothesis is partially refuted (feature_011, feature_022) but holds for the rest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 is an ordinal severity-style variable (likely performance status) with three levels (0,1,2), and pfs_months decreases monotonically as feature_001 increases.
feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is positively correlated with pfs_months (each unit increase corresponds to longer pfs_months).
Among the continuous features, feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_025 (continuous, range 0-25, mean 3.8) is negatively correlated with pfs_months (consistent with a tumor-burden style predictor).
feature_005 (continuous, range 0.02-777, highly right-skewed) has a small overall negative correlation with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_001: 0 -> 5.271 (n=17592), 1 -> 4.054 (n=24971), 2 -> 2.908 (n=7437). One-way ANOVA F=3528.9, p effectively 0. Monotone gradient confirmed.
Pearson r=+0.766, p effectively 0. Decile analysis shows mean pfs rises from 1.33 (lowest decile) to 7.45 (highest decile).
Pearson r=+0.102, p=3.3e-115. Modest but highly significant positive correlation.
Pearson r=-0.0377, p=3.1e-17. Small but significant overall negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on all 33 features will identify a small set of independently significant predictors (feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_005, feature_021, feature_027) and most lab-style continuous features (sodium, potassium, calcium, hemoglobin, creatinine, etc.) will be non-significant after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable OLS R^2=0.859. Independently significant (p<1e-3) predictors: feature_015 (+0.176/unit), feature_001 (-1.171/unit), feature_014 (-1.362), feature_006 (-0.287), feature_023 (-0.354), feature_022 (-0.357), feature_005 (-0.005/unit), feature_019 (+0.469/unit), feature_021 (-0.0004/unit), feature_025 (-0.076/unit), feature_027 (+0.943). All other features (feature_002, feature_003, feature_004, feature_007, feature_008, feature_009, feature_010, feature_011, feature_012, feature_013, feature_016, feature_017, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_026, feature_028, feature_029, feature_030, feature_031, feature_032, feature_033) p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, and feature_024 are randomized treatment-style assignments (independent of baseline severity), while feature_014, feature_001, feature_006, feature_023, and feature_022 are baseline clinical/biomarker characteristics.
If feature_027 is a randomized treatment, the joint distribution of feature_027 and feature_001 (severity) should be statistically independent.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df[T], df['feature_001'], normalize='index'); compare mean(feature_015) by T
analyses.result_summary: For feature_027, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024: feature_001 distribution and mean feature_015 are within 0.01-0.02 across T=0/T=1 (essentially identical), consistent with random allocation. Chi-square tests on these crosstabs all give p>0.2. We treat these six as candidate treatment indicators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the candidate treatments (feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_027), only feature_027 has a non-null marginal effect on pfs_months; the others have an estimated mean-difference indistinguishable from zero (|effect|<0.05 months, p>0.1).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months for T=1 vs T=0 for each candidate treatment.
analyses.result_summary: feature_027: +0.972, p=1.7e-216 (large). feature_010: -0.019, p=0.35. feature_012: +0.005, p=0.86. feature_018: +0.005, p=0.87. feature_020: -0.044, p=0.24. feature_024: -0.038, p=0.088. Hypothesis confirmed: only feature_027 has a meaningful marginal treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The marginal positive effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is heterogeneous: feature_027 effect is reduced or eliminated when feature_006=1 (interaction term feature_027*feature_006 is significantly negative).
The marginal positive effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is reduced or eliminated when feature_023=1 (interaction term feature_027*feature_023 is significantly negative).
The marginal positive effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is reduced or eliminated when feature_022=1 (interaction term feature_027*feature_022 is significantly negative).
feature_011=1 patients have an enhanced (more positive) feature_027 treatment effect compared to feature_011=0 patients.
Among continuous features, feature_005 negatively modifies the feature_027 treatment effect (interaction term feature_027*feature_005 is significantly negative).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.454, p=4.1e-168. Stratified: feature_027 effect at feature_006=0 -> +1.480 (p=4e-274); at feature_006=1 -> +0.026 (p=0.52). The treatment benefit is essentially abolished when feature_006=1.
Interaction coefficient = -1.629, p=7.7e-226. Stratified: feature_027 effect at feature_023=0 -> +1.656 (p=6e-293); at feature_023=1 -> +0.027 (p=0.46). Effect abolished when feature_023=1.
Interaction coefficient = -1.183, p=6.1e-23. Stratified: feature_027 effect at feature_022=0 -> +1.027 (p=5e-226); at feature_022=1 -> -0.156 (p=0.13). Effect abolished when feature_022=1.

Interaction coefficient = +0.988, p=1.2e-11 in marginal model. However the interaction disappears when feature_006/023/022 are also included, suggesting it reflects enrichment of feature_011=1 within the wild-type (feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_022=0) subgroup (53.5 vs 17.7 per thousand) rather than an independent effect modifier.
Interaction coefficient = -0.0253, p=8.7e-58. The treatment benefit decreases with increasing feature_005.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Of the candidate treatments other than feature_027, none shows a significant interaction (p<0.001) with any other feature - feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024 act as inert binary covariates.

analyses.code: For each candidate T in {feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024}, fit OLS pfs_months ~ T + f + T:f for every other feature f.
analyses.result_summary: feature_010: smallest interaction p = 0.022 (feature_022). feature_012: smallest p = 0.021 (feature_032). feature_018, feature_020, feature_024: no interaction p<0.05. None survives Bonferroni adjustment for ~30 features. These are not effect-modified treatments; they appear to be inert random binary indicators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 has a clinically and statistically meaningful PFS benefit only when feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 (jointly wild-type for both binary biomarkers); in any of the other three cells (006=1, 023=0), (006=0, 023=1), (006=1, 023=1), the feature_027 effect on pfs_months is null.

Adding feature_022=0 to the wild-type definition further enriches the responder subgroup: feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 patients have an even larger feature_027 effect than feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 alone.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests in 2x2 cells of feature_006 x feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 effect by (feature_006, feature_023): (0,0) -> +2.520 months, p effectively 0 (n=18853); (0,1) -> +0.061, p=0.17 (n=13490); (1,0) -> +0.071, p=0.16 (n=10249); (1,1) -> -0.040, p=0.52 (n=7408). Confirmed - benefit is concentrated only in the joint wild-type cell.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests across all 8 cells of (feature_006, feature_023, feature_022).
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 effect by (feature_006, feature_023, feature_022): (0,0,0) -> +2.758, p effectively 0 (n=17372); (0,0,1) -> -0.204, p=0.12; (0,1,0) -> +0.061, p=0.17; (1,0,0) -> +0.083, p=0.12; (1,0,1) -> -0.067, p=0.70; (1,1,0) -> -0.040, p=0.52. Triple wild-type definitively required for treatment benefit; feature_022=1 alone abolishes the effect even when 006 and 023 are 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple wild-type subgroup (feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0), feature_005 monotonically attenuates the feature_027 effect: PFS benefit declines steeply with rising feature_005 and approaches zero above some threshold.
Within the triple wild-type subgroup, the additional within-subgroup interaction screen will identify feature_005 (and only feature_005) as a continuous effect modifier of feature_027 at p<1e-50.

analyses.code: Decile analysis of feature_005 within WT3 subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Within WT3 (n=17372), feature_027 effect by feature_005 decile: deciles 0-4 (feature_005<=4.47): treatment effect ~+5.0 months (essentially flat plateau); decile 5 (4.48-6.02): +1.75; deciles 6-9 (>6): treatment effect ~0. Sharp threshold near feature_005=5.

Within WT3, interactions of feature_027 with each remaining feature: feature_005 dominates (int_coef=-0.0649, p=7.4e-136). feature_031 (+0.31, p=5.1e-4), feature_014 (+0.24, p=5.5e-3), feature_017 (+0.05, p=2.8e-2) reach nominal significance but are far weaker. feature_005 is by far the dominant within-WT3 modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining a binary 'responder' subgroup as (feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5) yields the cleanest single-threshold decomposition: outside this subgroup the feature_027 effect on pfs_months is statistically indistinguishable from zero; inside this subgroup the feature_027 effect on pfs_months is approximately +5.0 months.

analyses.code: Stratified Welch t-tests at the proposed cutoff; OLS pfs_months ~ T + responder + T:responder
analyses.result_summary: Inside responder (n=9338, treated=1905, untreated=7433): mean pfs_months on=9.103, off=4.089, diff=+5.014, p effectively 0. Outside (n=40662): on=4.147, off=4.123, diff=+0.024, p=0.36. Interaction model: T_x_responder coefficient = +4.99 (p=0; t=83.9). Hypothesis fully supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_005 cutoff is sharp at approximately 5.0 - shifting the cutoff up to 6 only modestly reduces effect (from +5.0 to +4.5), but shifting up to 7 reduces the effect substantially because the population beyond the threshold has true zero benefit.

Each of feature_006, feature_023, feature_022 is individually necessary for the responder definition: a patient who is wild-type for two of them but mutant for the third (with low feature_005) does NOT derive feature_027 benefit.

analyses.code: Threshold sweep on feature_005 within WT3.
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 effect within (WT3 AND feature_005<=thr): thr=3 -> +5.03 (low); thr=4 -> +5.04; thr=5 -> +5.01; thr=6 -> +4.54; thr=7 -> +4.13; thr=8 -> +3.90; thr=10 -> +3.61. The effect within (WT3 AND feature_005>thr) is essentially zero at every threshold from 5 upward (-0.002 to +0.018). Hypothesis supported - the benefit shuts off near feature_005=5-6.

feature_006=1 / others=0 / feature_005<=5: diff=+0.113 (n=5154). feature_023=1 / others=0 / low5: diff=+0.068 (n=7274). feature_022=1 / others=0 / low5: diff=-0.165 (n=779). All small and not statistically meaningful. Each marker is individually necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Off-treatment (feature_027=0) PFS distributions across (responder vs non-responder) subgroups are essentially identical, confirming that the responder subgroup is not simply a more prognostically favorable cohort but truly defines treatment-effect modification.

analyses.code: Mean pfs_months for feature_027=0 patients within each (WT3, low_feature_005) cell.
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_027=0 patients: WT3=0, low5=0 -> mean pfs=4.107; WT3=0, low5=1 -> 4.124; WT3=1, low5=0 -> 4.149; WT3=1, low5=1 -> 4.089. Differences <0.07 months across cells. The biomarker subgroup is purely an effect-modification subgroup, not a prognostic one.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the universal prognostic factors (feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025), the responder-specific feature_027 effect estimate remains approximately +5 months, and the main feature_027 effect collapses to near zero (consistent with all benefit being contained in the responder subgroup).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ T + responder + T:responder + feature_015 + feature_001 + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025 + feature_006 + feature_023 + feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model R^2=0.993. T main effect coef=-0.004 (p=0.11) - effectively zero. T:responder coef=+4.982 (p=0). Prognostic features: feature_015 +0.176 (p=0), feature_001 -1.166 (p=0), feature_014 -1.368 (p=0), feature_019 +0.485 (p=0), feature_025 -0.078 (p=0). feature_006/023/022 main effects all near zero (p>0.27) - they only matter through their interaction with feature_027.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (likely performance status) does not modify the feature_027 treatment effect within the responder subgroup - the +5 month benefit is consistent across feature_001=0, 1, and 2.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests within responder by feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: Within responder, feature_027 effect by feature_001: 0 -> +4.870; 1 -> +5.091; 2 -> +4.812. The treatment benefit is approximately constant (within 0.3 months) across performance-status levels. feature_001 is prognostic but not predictive within responder.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011, feature_028, feature_002, feature_016 (the rare binary markers) are not effect modifiers of feature_027 once the WT3 + low feature_005 responder definition is applied.

analyses.code: Within responder, t-tests stratified by each rare binary marker.
analyses.result_summary: Within responder (n=9338): feature_011=0 effect=+5.001 (n=8843), feature_011=1 effect=+5.235 (n=495). The earlier +0.99 marginal interaction of feature_011 with feature_027 was an artifact of feature_011 being enriched in the WT3 subset (5.4 percent vs 1.8 percent). Within responder, feature_011 has no additional modifying role. Other rare markers similarly inert.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (the strongest prognostic continuous feature, r=+0.77) does not modify the feature_027 treatment effect: its interaction p-value is non-significant (>0.05) in the full population.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.0017, p=0.28. feature_015 is purely prognostic, not predictive of feature_027 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (PS) does not modify the feature_027 treatment effect in the full population either (interaction p>0.05).
feature_014 does not modify the feature_027 treatment effect in the full population (interaction p>0.05).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.0047, p=0.89. feature_001 is purely prognostic.
Interaction coef = +0.0696, p=0.15. feature_014 is purely prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014, the largest binary prognostic factor (mean diff -1.36), is independent of treatment assignment (feature_027) in the data - consistent with random treatment allocation.
The biomarker feature_006, feature_023, feature_022 are independent of treatment assignment (feature_027) - consistent with stratified random allocation.

analyses.code: Crosstabs and chi-square between feature_027 and each of feature_014, feature_006, feature_023, feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: Joint distributions are within ~1% of expected proportions; chi-square p-values all >0.2. Treatment is balanced over all five baseline factors (feature_001, feature_014, feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_015, feature_019, etc.).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the lab-style continuous features (feature_003, feature_004, feature_007, feature_008, feature_009, feature_013, feature_017, feature_021, feature_026, feature_029, feature_030, feature_032, feature_033), none has a meaningful (|r|>0.05) Pearson correlation with pfs_months in the full cohort.

analyses.code: Pearson r for each continuous feature.
analyses.result_summary: All listed features have |r|<0.013 except feature_005 (r=-0.038) and feature_021 (r=-0.011, p=0.013). The labs are essentially uninformative about pfs_months at the cohort level. Only feature_015, feature_019, feature_025, feature_005 carry continuous-feature signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the OFF-treatment (feature_027=0) cohort has the same prognostic structure as the overall off-treatment cohort - higher feature_015 and lower feature_001/feature_014 still predict longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS within responder, feature_027=0, regress pfs on feature_015, feature_001, feature_014.
analyses.result_summary: Within responder/off-treatment (n=7433): feature_015 coef +0.175 (p=0), feature_001 -1.165 (p=0), feature_014 -1.367 (p=0). Same magnitudes as full cohort - confirms that the responder subgroup carries normal prognostic structure off treatment, and the gap between on/off arms is the treatment effect itself.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the ON-treatment (feature_027=1) cohort shows the same prognostic relationships, with an additive shift of approximately +5 months on top.

analyses.code: OLS within responder, feature_027=1; compare intercept and prognostic coefficients to off-treatment regression.
analyses.result_summary: Within responder/on-treatment (n=1905): feature_015 +0.175, feature_001 -1.166, feature_014 -1.366; intercept shifted by +4.98 vs off-treatment. The treatment provides a near-constant additive PFS shift in the responder subgroup, independent of prognostic background.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint heterogeneity tree: the optimal feature_027-effect-defining subgroup uses 4 features in this nested order - (feature_023=0) AND (feature_006=0) AND (feature_022=0) AND (feature_005<=5) - and these are jointly necessary; no smaller subset achieves the +5 month benefit on its own.

analyses.code: Compare all sub-definitions of the responder subgroup; verify that dropping any single condition reduces the effect substantially.
analyses.result_summary: Effect by definition: WT3 alone (n=17372) -> +2.758. WT3 AND low5 (n=9338) -> +5.014. Dropping feature_006: (023=0 AND 022=0 AND low5) (n=14492) -> +3.84. Dropping feature_023: (006=0 AND 022=0 AND low5) (n=15528) -> +3.59. Dropping feature_022: (006=0 AND 023=0 AND low5) (n=10117) -> +4.65. Dropping low5: (006=0 AND 023=0 AND 022=0) (n=17372) -> +2.76. The 4-condition subgroup uniquely achieves ~+5 months; each component contributes substantially.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An alternative (continuous) characterization is that the feature_027 PFS benefit equals approximately (5 months) * I(feature_006=0) * I(feature_023=0) * I(feature_022=0) * max(0, 1 - feature_005/5), i.e. a multiplicative gating of three binary biomarker conditions and a ramp-down on feature_005.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ T + WT3 + feature_005 + T:WT3 + T:feature_005 + WT3:feature_005 + T:WT3:feature_005.
analyses.result_summary: T (main) coef = +0.016 (p=0.65). T:WT3 = +3.330 (p=0). T:WT3:feature_005 = -0.0665 (p=0). T:feature_005 outside WT3 = +0.0015 (p=0.43). Confirms that the dose-response on feature_005 only operates within WT3. Multiplicative gating model is consistent with the data.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL responder subgroup: patients with feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5 derive a feature_027 treatment benefit of approximately +5.0 months pfs_months; in all other patients the feature_027 effect on pfs_months is essentially zero (point estimate +0.02, 95% CI roughly [-0.03, +0.07]).

analyses.result_summary: In responder (n=9338): mean PFS treated 9.103, untreated 4.089; difference +5.014 months, 95% CI [+4.91, +5.12]. Outside responder (n=40662): mean PFS treated 4.147, untreated 4.123; difference +0.024 months, 95% CI [-0.03, +0.07]. Interaction coefficient 4.99 (p=0). The biomarker-defined subgroup completely captures all of feature_027's treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Summary prognostic-vs-predictive map for ds001_crc: PROGNOSTIC features that affect pfs_months independent of treatment - feature_015 (strong positive, +0.18/unit), feature_001 (negative gradient ~-1.17/level), feature_014 (negative ~-1.37), feature_019 (positive +0.49/unit), feature_025 (negative -0.076/unit), feature_021 (negative -0.0004/unit). PREDICTIVE features that modify feature_027 treatment effect - feature_006, feature_023, feature_022 (each a binary on/off gate), feature_005 (continuous threshold). NEUTRAL features - feature_002, feature_010, feature_011, feature_012, feature_016, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_028, feature_031, and the lab panel feature_003/004/007/008/009/013/017/026/029/030/032/033.

analyses.result_summary: The full population's pfs_months variation is well-explained (R^2=0.993) by an additive prognostic model of {feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025} plus a single multiplicative treatment-effect term: 4.98 * I(feature_006=0) * I(feature_023=0) * I(feature_022=0) * I(feature_005<=5) * feature_027. This single subgroup-defined treatment effect is the only substantial source of treatment-related heterogeneity in this dataset.
